title: URL Space Traps, Structural Fingerprints, Soft Errors, and Stop Evidence · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in trap detection and bounded containment policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and ev…
objectives: Detect temporal, pagination, facet, search, session, nonce, sort, repeated-segment, depth, and parameter-combination expansion.
Bound distinct path templates, query keys, values, combinations, depths, and pages per class with owned exceptions.
Combine status, redirect, title, main text, structure, known markers, and repetition to classify soft error and challenge pages.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Trap Features
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 6816 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to contain calendar, query, session, structure, and soft-error traps with bounded incident evidence; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Detect temporal, pagination, facet, search, session, nonce, sort, repeated-segment, depth, and parameter-combination expansion.

Inspect the Trap Features trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Trap Features.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for detect temporal, pagination, facet, search, session, nonce, sort, repeated-segment, depth, and parameter-combination expansion. For Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Trap Features, keep Trap Features connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Only exact duplicate links cause crawl loops.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect temporal, pagination, facet, search, session, nonce, sort, repeated-segment, depth, and parameter-combination expansion. Artifact under review: trap detection and bounded containment policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Trap Features, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Trap Features record says trap detection and bounded containment policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Trap Features.

For inspect the failure record, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Trap Features for trap detection and bounded containment policy.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Containment Incident
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-containment-incident-8vc2on" that uses case 5391 to contain calendar, query, session, structure, and soft-error traps with bounded incident evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Containment Incident.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Containment Incident into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Containment Incident, keep Containment Incident connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Containment Incident structural check is only one gate. trap detection and bounded containment policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Another successful Containment Incident run of the same trap detection and bounded containment policy happy path. That claim fails the containment incident · fault isolation evidence boundary for Containment Incident.
